Document:
[color=#ffff00][head=3]Section 10: Escalation Guildelines[/head][/color]
[color=#a4885c]10.1[/color] Escalate conflicts reasonably. Use common sense when deciding how far a situation should go. A spilled drink might lead to an argument or fistfight, but should not escalate into a gunfight. ★★
[color=#a4885c]10.2[/color] When instigating, talk and posture first. Threaten with violence, brandish a weapon, or even fire a warning shot before engaging in combat. ★
[color=#a4885c]10.2A[/color] If you are Command, Security, TDF, or an Antagonist, you are expected to have and attempt to use some form of non-lethal force before resorting to lethal force. If you are none of those roles and have access to non-lethal options, you should still attempt to use them.
[color=#a4885c]10.2B[/color] If someone boards your ship, escalation rules are more lenient for the purpose of removing them from your ship.
[color=#a4885c]10.3[/color] Accept surrender if it is offered, regardless of how heated the situation is. ★
[color=#a4885c]10.3A[/color] Do not intentionally offer a false surrender. ★★
[color=#a4885c]10.4[/color] Do not intentionally round-remove players. If you kill someone, you are not expected to revive them, but you must not take any actions that would prevent Medical from reviving them. ★★
